source_files: Fremantle 2025 Segmentation AI-Ready Format 2026_05 V2.xlsx (sheets: Persona Info, Breezy Boomers)

profile.Profile: Breezy Boomers fans are confident and easygoing consumers who enjoy the rewards of a stable financial situation. They gravitate toward brands that offer convenience, reliability, and a sense of familiarity, favouring products or services that enhance their leisure-focused lifestyle. Tech-capable but not overly dependent on digital trends, they appreciate straightforward communication and value-for-money offerings that cater to their preferences for comfort and quality.
profile.Consumer Attributes:
"Breezy Boomer" fans:
Relaxed and Confident: Approaches life with a laid-back attitude, valuing comfort and ease in their choices.
Financially Stable: Likely to have a steady income or retirement savings, allowing for discretionary spending on leisure and quality.
Experience-Oriented: Prefers enjoyable experiences over material possessions, such as travel, dining, or social activities.
Value-Conscious: Looks for good value without compromising on quality, favouring trusted and reliable brands.
Cautiously Tech-Capable: Comfortable using technology for convenience (e.g., online shopping or smart devices) but approaches new tech trends with caution, preferring familiar tools and platforms.
Health and Wellness Aware: Interested in products and services that promote well-being, comfort, and an active lifestyle.
Community-Focused: Enjoys being part of social groups or engaging in community events, whether locally or through online communities.
Appreciates Simplicity: Favours straightforward, no-frills communication and solutions that align with their relaxed, low-stress outlook.
profile.Bio: Robert and Susan are Fremantle to the core and love being part of the Purple Army, with Robert now a 20+ year member. They live a comfortable and indulgent lifestyle having downsized to a low maintenance townhouse close to cafes, restaurants, museums, galleries and the theatre. With ample time and financial resources from superannuation and a healthy share portfolio, they enjoy going to the football while also appreciating the finer things in life. Now that their kids are young adults and have left the nest, they have the freedom to explore their passions and interests which include good food and wine and embracing the arts and they are regular visitors to Perth's Cultural Centre. Robert comes from a large Italian family and enjoys nothing more than celebrating family events over big Italian lunches at restaurants like La Sosta (one of his favourite places) and talking up Freo's chances of winning a flag.
profile.About Me:
Being able to support Fremantle as member is important to me.
When it comes to new technology, I am not an early adopter.
I'm proud of my heritage and culture.
When I buy, I'm looking for good quality at a fair price.
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me.

profile.Personal Attitudes: I think Australia is the best country in the world; I understand what's going on with news and current affairs; I am satisfied with what I have and don't want more; Getting ahead in should be strictly based on merit; I obey the rules, even when no one is watching; I believe Australia should do more to upgrade its defence capabilities; I think immigration to Australia should be strictly limited; I believes that we all have a responsibility to save the natural environment; I make the security of my home and neighbourhood a top priority; I am in a financially secure position; I'm a moderate, reasonable and avoid extremes

profile.Cultural Diversity: Moderate: The Breezy Boomer have a moderate level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of second-generation Australians with a European heritage particularly Italy and Greece. The Breezy Boomers have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base and index high on females. Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
profile.Sustainability: Moderate: Breezy Boomers fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and are reasonably proactive in supporting Sustainability initiatives. They invest in solar panels, conserve water, recycle diligently, and take particular care around household chemicals - reading labels carefully and disposing of hazardous products responsibly. Breezy Boomers can often be seen volunteering for community clean-up events near where they live.
profile.Political Values: Conservative: Breezy Boomers fans hold strong and settled political views. Firmly conservative in outlook, they back parties that reflect their core beliefs - individual freedom, private enterprise, and a robust national defence are non-negotiable priorities for this segment.
profile.Outlook on Life: Slightly Negative to Neutral: The Breezy Boomers have a neutral Outlook on Life, trending towards slightly negative. They see themselves as generally a bit better off than most but are not overly positive about the future even though they are generally good in health. Breezy Boomers expect little to change from the way things are for themselves and the world.

knowledge.title: Identity & demographics
knowledge.text:
Persona: Breezy Boomers (representative members: Robert & Susan)
Profile: Breezy Boomers fans are confident and easygoing consumers who enjoy the rewards of a stable financial situation. They gravitate toward brands that offer convenience, reliability, and a sense of familiarity, favouring products or services that enhance their leisure-focused lifestyle. Tech-capable but not overly dependent on digital trends, they appreciate straightforward communication and value-for-money offerings that cater to their preferences for comfort and quality.
Consumer attributes: "Breezy Boomer" fans:
Relaxed and Confident: Approaches life with a laid-back attitude, valuing comfort and ease in their choices.
Financially Stable: Likely to have a steady income or retirement savings, allowing for discretionary spending on leisure and quality.
Experience-Oriented: Prefers enjoyable experiences over material possessions, such as travel, dining, or social activities.
Value-Conscious: Looks for good value without compromising on quality, favouring trusted and reliable brands.
Cautiously Tech-Capable: Comfortable using technology for convenience (e.g., online shopping or smart devices) but approaches new tech trends with caution, preferring familiar tools and platforms.
Health and Wellness Aware: Interested in products and services that promote well-being, comfort, and an active lifestyle.
Community-Focused: Enjoys being part of social groups or engaging in community events, whether locally or through online communities.
Appreciates Simplicity: Favours straightforward, no-frills communication and solutions that align with their relaxed, low-stress outlook.

knowledge.title: Bio - Robert & Susan
knowledge.text: Robert and Susan are Fremantle to the core and love being part of the Purple Army, with Robert now a 20+ year member. They live a comfortable and indulgent lifestyle having downsized to a low maintenance townhouse close to cafes, restaurants, museums, galleries and the theatre. With ample time and financial resources from superannuation and a healthy share portfolio, they enjoy going to the football while also appreciating the finer things in life. Now that their kids are young adults and have left the nest, they have the freedom to explore their passions and interests which include good food and wine and embracing the arts and they are regular visitors to Perth's Cultural Centre. Robert comes from a large Italian family and enjoys nothing more than celebrating family events over big Italian lunches at restaurants like La Sosta (one of his favourite places) and talking up Freo's chances of winning a flag.

knowledge.title: About me (first-person voice)
knowledge.text:
Being able to support Fremantle as member is important to me.
When it comes to new technology, I am not an early adopter.
I'm proud of my heritage and culture.
When I buy, I'm looking for good quality at a fair price.
Being able to enjoy the finer things in life is important to me.

knowledge.title: Cultural, sustainability & outlook
knowledge.text:
Cultural diversity: Moderate: The Breezy Boomer have a moderate level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of second-generation Australians with a European heritage particularly Italy and Greece. The Breezy Boomers have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base and index high on females. Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
Sustainability: Moderate: Breezy Boomers fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and are reasonably proactive in supporting Sustainability initiatives. They invest in solar panels, conserve water, recycle diligently, and take particular care around household chemicals - reading labels carefully and disposing of hazardous products responsibly. Breezy Boomers can often be seen volunteering for community clean-up events near where they live.
Political values: Conservative: Breezy Boomers fans hold strong and settled political views. Firmly conservative in outlook, they back parties that reflect their core beliefs - individual freedom, private enterprise, and a robust national defence are non-negotiable priorities for this segment.
Outlook on life: Slightly Negative to Neutral: The Breezy Boomers have a neutral Outlook on Life, trending towards slightly negative. They see themselves as generally a bit better off than most but are not overly positive about the future even though they are generally good in health. Breezy Boomers expect little to change from the way things are for themselves and the world.

knowledge.title: Membership movement 2024-2025
knowledge.text:
Breezy Boomers
Commercial In Confidence
We're Robert & Susan...
The Breezy Boomers segment increased in net numbers from 2024 by 16%, through a combination of movements from other segments and solid growth in new and/or returning members.
There was good growth in new members and /or returning lapsed members which was significantly more than the volume of members who did not renew.
Churn of members was 10%, which is much lower than the club baseline (22%) and lowest of all segments. Further analysis is required to determine what % of churn should be categorised as attrition. 
Membership Movements from 2024 - 2025

Breezy Boomers
Commercial In Confidence
Breezy Boomers fans are confident and easygoing consumers who enjoy the rewards of a stable financial situation. They gravitate toward brands that offer convenience, reliability, and a sense of familiarity, favouring products or services that enhance their leisure-focused lifestyle. Tech-capable but not overly dependent on digital trends, they appreciate straightforward communication and value-for-money offerings that cater to their preferences for comfort and quality.
We're Robert & Susan...
"Breezy Boomer" fans:
Relaxed and Confident: Approaches life with a laid-back attitude, valuing comfort and ease in their choices.
Financially Stable: Likely to have a steady income or retirement savings, allowing for discretionary spending on leisure and quality.
Experience-Oriented: Prefers enjoyable experiences over material possessions, such as travel, dining, or social activities.
Value-Conscious: Looks for good value without compromising on quality, favouring trusted and reliable brands.
Cautiously Tech-Capable: Comfortable using technology for convenience (e.g., online shopping or smart devices) but approaches new tech trends with caution, preferring familiar tools and platforms.
Health and Wellness Aware: Interested in products and services that promote well-being, comfort, and an active lifestyle.
Community-Focused: Enjoys being part of social groups or engaging in community events, whether locally or through online communities.
Appreciates Simplicity: Favours straightforward, no-frills communication and solutions that align with their relaxed, low-stress outlook.

Breezy Boomers
Commercial In Confidence
We're Robert & Susan...
The Breezy Boomers segment increased in net numbers from 2024 by 16%, through a combination of movements from other segments and solid growth in new and/or returning members.
There was good growth in new members and /or returning lapsed members which was significantly more than the volume of members who did not renew.
Churn of members was 10%, which is much lower than the club baseline (22%) and lowest of all segments. Further analysis is required to determine what % of churn should be categorised as attrition. 
Membership Movements from 2024 - 2025

We're Robert & Susan...
BIO
Robert and Susan are Fremantle to the core and love being part of the Purple Army, with Robert now a 20+ year member. They live a comfortable and indulgent lifestyle having downsized to a low maintenance townhouse close to cafes, restaurants, museums, galleries and the theatre. With ample time and financial resources from superannuation and a healthy share portfolio, they enjoy going to the football while also appreciating the finer things in life. Now that their kids are young adults and have left the nest, they have the freedom to explore their passions and interests which include good food and wine and embracing the arts and they are regular visitors to Perth's Cultural Centre. Robert comes from a large Italian family and enjoys nothing more than celebrating family events over big Italian lunches at restaurants like La Sosta (one of his favourite places) and talking up Freo's chances of winning a flag. 
Breezy Boomers

Breezy Boomers
Commercial In Confidence
Values and Attitudes Dashboard
Cultural Diversity
Sustainability
Political Values
Outlook on Life
The Breezy Boomer have a moderate level of cultural diversity with a strong representation of second-generation Australians with a European heritage particularly Italy and Greece. The Breezy Boomers have a higher percentage of males to the Australian population but slightly less than the Fremantle member base and index high on females. Religious affiliations are less diverse with a bias towards Christianity.
Moderate
Moderate 
Conservative
Breezy Boomers fans are aware of the importance of Sustainability and are reasonably proactive in supporting Sustainability initiatives. They invest in solar panels, conserve water, recycle diligently, and take particular care around household chemicals - reading labels carefully and disposing of hazardous products responsibly. Breezy Boomers can often be seen volunteering for community clean-up events near where they live.
Breezy Boomers fans hold strong and settled political views. Firmly conservative in outlook, they back parties that reflect their core beliefs - individual freedom, private enterprise, and a robust national defence are non-negotiable priorities for this segment.
The Breezy Boomers have a neutral Outlook on Life, trending towards slightly negative. They see themselves as generally a bit better off than most but are not overly positive about the future even though they are generally good in health. Breezy Boomers expect little to change from the way things are for themselves and the world. 
The Values and Attitudes Dashboard provides a general overview of this fan segment's views and feelings towards sustainability, their outlook on life, politics and the cultural diversity of the segment.
Cultural Diversity refers to differences among people because of their racial or ethnic backgrounds, language, dress and traditions. The term cultural diversity can also refer to having different cultures respect each other's differences.
Sustainability means a focus on meeting our needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. Sustainability is not just environmentalism it also includes concerns for social equity and economic development.
Political values are what people believe in that relates to how government should operate, whom it should serve or not serve, who pays for it and who benefits. This is normally referenced by a tendency to a political ideology.
Outlook on Life relates to a person's way of looking at things, their 'point of view' or attitude and how they perceive their life now and how positive they are towards the future, and the factors that impact the way they see the future. 
Slightly Negative to Neutral

membership opportunities
Retention-Dependent Growth
The continued growth of Modern Families and Happy Suburbanites is essential for Fremantle's long-term membership stability.
As members age, they naturally migrate into Comfortable Crowd, Founding Faithfuls, or Breezy Boomers. Without strong retention in these middle-life segments, the pipeline into older loyal groups weakens.
Natural Decline of Older Cohorts
Segments like Breezy Boomers, Founding Faithfuls and (to a lesser extent) Comfortable Crowd include members reaching the later stages of the membership lifecycle and will experience gradual attrition over the next 10-20 years.
Unless actively replenished from younger feeder segments, the club risks a decline in overall stability and lifetime value.
Gen Fun & Urban Hipsters Can't Do It Alone
Despite good growth rates and strong inflows, both Gen Fun and Urban Hipsters are marked by higher churn percentages suggesting lower stickiness.
These segments are vital feeders into Fremantle's family and mid-life tiers but require sustained investment to improve retention and long-term engagement.
Experience-building and early value delivery will be key to ensuring these members don't lapse before progressing through the lifecycle.

key observations
Urban Hipsters are the most dynamic and volatile segment, with the highest churn and migration out but also the largest intake of new members. Despite strong recruitment, the segment experienced a net contraction, reinforcing the need for personalised onboarding and early engagement.
Modern Families and Happy Suburbanites are crucial growth bridges, showing high inbound migration and strong overall net growth. These are the engine rooms of Fremantle's mid-lifecycle fan base, benefiting from both migration and new acquisition.
Gen Fun is a high-potential development segment, with the second-highest new member rate. With a net growth, this segment is critical for capturing early fandom and building identity but requires nurture to avoid early drop-off.
Founding Faithfuls is a unique hybrid segment - skewing older but also housing younger adults in multi-generational households. It is one of Fremantle's most stable cohorts, with low migration and moderate churn, making it a reliable membership anchor. Its strength lies in loyalty and continuity, rather than growth - and it plays a vital role in maintaining generational links with the club.
Breezy Boomers is a stable, late-lifecycle segment, with modest churn and minimal migration out. Their net growth is largely supported by strong in-flows from Comfortable Crowd and Happy Suburbanites, rather than new member acquisition.

New Member segment
Gen Fun represents a key entry point into the Fremantle Football Club - attracting younger members transitioning into adulthood, often the children of long-standing members from segments like Breezy Boomers, and Comfortable Crowd.
The largest growth in percentage terms of all segments. Although it's a newer segment, churn is very high. This suggests a strong base of early interest - but one that needs deliberate nurturing to build loyalty and embed fandom into their social identity.
With significantly different lifestyle and consumption habits, being digital native, this segment requires a more nuanced approach to secure ongoing engagement. Their use of traditional communications channels is low and requires a strong digital engagement model.
Outlook: A small but emerging segment with high lifetime value potential. It's crucial for early capture but loses loyalty fast unless deliberately cultivated and developed.

members on the move
Anchor segment
Happy Suburbanite members primarily arrive from Modern Families and Urban Hipster segments, but they also flow onward into older, more established groups like Comfortable Crowd (which is slightly older), Breezy Boomers and potentially Founding Faithful. While churn is relatively low, migration in and out is high - particularly inbound.
This is a transitional middle-aged segment that carries high financial and strategic value. It plays a dual role: contributing meaningfully to the club now, while also nurturing the next generation of long-term loyal fans.
Outlook: Members don't typically stay here long-term; it's a pipeline segment into Fremantle's older, more rusted-on supporter groups. Nonetheless, it remains critical for growing and stabilising the base during key life-stage transitions.

members on the move
'End of Cycle' segments
As Happy Suburbanites and Comfortable Crowd segments approach the latter stages of their membership lifecycle. Both act as feeder groups into the Breezy Boomer segment - Fremantle's oldest and most stable profile.
These are highly loyal and sticky cohorts, with minimal outflows and strong long-term retention. However, over the next 10 -15 years, they are expected to face natural attrition due to aging, retirement, and eventual lifecycle exit.
2025 saw an increase in "new" members, of which it is expected that many are returning members, and not brand-new members to the club.
While Founding Faithfuls have some older members, it is unlike other older-skewing segments, Founding Faithfuls display stronger retention and age diversity, making it more of a stable base than an end-of-cycle group.
Outlook: These segments offer short-to-medium term stability and reliable value but will naturally decline over time unless deliberately replenished. Strategies to honour their loyalty while activating the next generation of fans will be critical.

The Fremantle member base is growing, diverse, and increasingly polarised between loyalty and volatility. Seven segments show meaningfully different profiles across tenure, attendance, churn and commercial value. Overall membership grew in 2025, driven by strong new member acquisition across multiple segments - but this growth co-exists with a club baseline churn rate of 22% and a highest-segment churn rate of 47% (Urban Hipsters), creating a pattern of high-volume churn and high-volume replacement that is commercially inefficient and structurally fragile.
Value is concentrated in older, longer-tenured segments. Breezy Boomers and Comfortable Crowd carry the highest average value, and the longest average tenures. Their stability is real but finite - their contribution will naturally decline over time unless mid-lifecycle segments are actively retained and converted.
Engagement does not reliably track with value or tenure. Gen Fun shows the highest new member growth but the second-highest churn. Urban Hipsters attract the largest volume of new members but also recorded a net contraction - the only segment to do so. Email engagement ranges from 52% open rates in Breezy Boomers to just 19% in Gen Fun and 32% in Urban Hipsters, reinforcing that broadcast communications are ineffective across the segments that need them most.
Fremantle members over-index on commercially significant spend categories. Spend propensity data shows clear alignment across EV charging and vehicles, financial services, travel, premium dining, and insurance - categories that support targeted, evidence-based partner propositions that move the club beyond generic audience reach.
summary of findings

key observations
These observations surface the behaviours that matter most for driving retention, value growth and commercial outcomes across the Fremantle Dockers member base.
Engagement
Email performance is weakest precisely where it matters most. Gen Fun members open just 19% of emails and click at 2% - the lowest of all segments and a clear signal that broadcast communications are not reaching this cohort with relevant content or timing.
One in four members with game access attended no events in 2025. This non-attending cohort is concentrated in younger segments and is behaviourally indistinct from lapsed members - the difference is they are still paying.
Churn Risk
Urban Hipsters churned at 47% - more than double the club baseline of 22% - and recorded the only net contraction of any segment. New member volume is masking an underlying retention failure that will become harder to paper over as acquisition efficiency declines.
Gen Fun grew by 53% in net terms but carried 27% churn. High headline growth and high churn in the same segment is a warning sign: the base is being rebuilt annually rather than compounded.
Loyalty & Tenure
Breezy Boomers and Founding Faithfuls members are the club's retention anchors - both grew in 2025, driven by in-migration and securing new and returning lapsed members. Their churn rates are the two lowest of all segments. Their value is structural, not scalable.
Members who reach 10+ years tenure show dramatically lower churn propensity. The data consistently points to years 0-4 as the period that determines whether a new member becomes a long-term asset or a replacement cost.
Value & Share of Wallet
AMPPI indexing reveals a two-tier commercial base. Breezy Boomers and Comfortable Crowd sit at high value; Urban Hipsters and Gen Fun are the lowest-value segments and are also the highest-churn segments - a combination that makes them costly to retain and difficult to monetise.
Fremantle members over-index on EV, financial services, travel and premium lifestyle spend. This presents a targetable commercial profile that supports segment-aligned partnership propositions rather than broad reach conversations.

recommendations
Product Planning | Treat membership products as lifecycle-stage solutions. Modern Families need flexible, family-oriented bundles. Gen Fun respond to experiential and social framing. Breezy Boomers and Founding Faithfuls value tenure recognition and belonging.
Develop a non-access tier with genuine digital and content value to retain the growing cohort of members who cannot or do not attend before they lapse entirely..
Increasing Engagement | Increase segment-led communications - different content, cadence and channel for each segment. Gen Fun's 19% open rate and 2% click rate are not a content problem; they are a segmentation problem.
Use engagement scoring to direct effort toward high-churn, low-engagement segments. Rewarding already-secure members is the least efficient use of activation investment.
Increasing ROI for Existing Partners | Move partners from reach-based to segment-aligned activation. Spend propensity data enables clear narratives: a financial services partner aligned to Comfortable Crowd and Breezy Boomers reaches members actively researching investments, wealth management and premium insurance.
Give partners segment-level activation briefs connecting who they are reaching, why it matters commercially, and how performance is measured - not an audience headcount.
Winning New Partners | Use the segmentation as a commercial asset. Four segments over-index on EV charging and vehicle spend, with Comfortable Crowd at 3.2x Tesla propensity - a precise, evidence-based proposition for automotive and energy transition brands.
Category Profile Reports across financial services, travel and active lifestyle enable Fremantle to approach non-traditional sponsors with audience precision rather than audience size.
